raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, while in XXXX XXXX XXXX, I was XXXX  and visited a business operating as XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX ( aka XXXX XXXX ). I was charged XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$400.00} USD ) on my American Express card ending in XXXX for what I believed were 3 cocktails and one XXXX XXXX. I was never informed of prices upfront, and never purchased the items listed on the submitted receipt ( including 2 bottles of XXXX XXXX 

I did not sign any receipt, nor was I shown one during the transaction. Later, the merchant submitted to AMEX a falsified charge acceptance form and an itemized receipt with a forged signature. I believe they took unauthorized photos of my ID and credit card to forge these documents after the fact. 

I submitted a sworn statement and police report to AMEX, but they reversed the original favorable outcome after the forged receipt was submitted. Numerous online reviews report similar cases of tourists being overcharged, having cards cloned, and IDs photographed without consent at this business. 

This is identity misuse and consumer fraud. The merchant acted deceptively and submitted fraudulent documents in an attempt to override my valid dispute.
